quoteFirst: I had exerted myself very much during the day, and had not eaten anything since breakfast. I reported to Elder Rae the situation of the folks behind. He immediately got up some horses, and the boys from the valley started back
quoteLast: to help the ox-teams in. The night was very severe, and many of the animals were frozen. It was five A.M. before the last team reached the camp.
title: The Lions of the Lord: A Tale of the Old West
author: Wilson, Harry Leon, 1867-1939

quoteFirst: That was also found on the stairs, as if it had dropped from the pocket of some man as he went down. And it is proved--indeed, your father tells me so--that he went to that house last night and did not leave it until
title: Brooke's Daughter: A Novel
author: Sergeant, Adeline, 1851-1904

quoteLast: when a friend came to inform Robert that the king's men had procured a warrant against him for resisting his majesty's officers, and he must fly for his life. There was a flutter of hushed excitement. Everybody was awakened.
title: A Century Too Soon: The Age of Tyranny
author: Musick, John R. (John Roy), 1849-1901; Carter, F. A. (Freeland A.), 1856-1948 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: We swung at anchor there until Mr. Morland came aboard with his friends, and we left on the turn of the tide
quoteLast: . I did not see Mr. Morland arrive, as I was busy in the forecastle with a man who had met with a trivial accident. It was Lane who informed me that the "butterflies were come" and we might spread our wings.
title: Hurricane Island
author: Watson, H. B. Marriott (Henry Brereton Marriott), 1863-1921

quoteFirst: And therewith he clothed him anon, and start upon his feet out of his bed as an whole man, and thanked Our Lord that He had healed him. And that was not to the world ward, for anon he yielded him to a place of religion of white monks, and was a full holy man. That same night
quoteLast: came a voice among them which said: My sons and not my chief sons, my friends and not my warriors, go ye hence where ye hope best to do and as I bad you. Ah, thanked be Thou, Lord, that Thou wilt vouchsafe to call us, Thy sinners.
title:
Chronicle and Romance: Froissart, Malory, Holinshed

author: Froissart, Jean, 1338?-1410? [Contributor]; Berners, John Bourchier, Lord, 1467?-1533 [Contributor]; Harrison, William, 1534-1593 [Contributor]; Malory, Thomas, Sir, -1471 [Contributor]; Caxton, William, 1422?-1492? [Editor]; Macaulay, G. C. (George Campbell), 1852-1915 [Editor]

quoteFirst: In addition to this he worked on the farm, acted as hostler, ground corn, built the fires, put the water early on the fire, and prepared for the mistress’s cooking. Though he was obliged to rise so early, he always studied till
quoteLast: . He was in great demand when hogs were slaughtered. For this rough work he was paid 31 cents (about 16d.) a-day. Meanwhile, he became incredibly strong.
title: Abraham Lincoln and the Abolition of Slavery in the United States
author: Leland, Charles Godfrey, 1824-1903

quoteLast: when Dansowich was awakened from a deep but troubled slumber by a grating noise at the door of his dungeon. Anxiety of mind, and still more, the effect of confinement in an impure and stifling atmosphere, upon one accustomed to the breezes of the Adriatic and the free air of the mountains, had impaired his health, and his sleep was broken by harassing and painful dreams.
title: Blackwood's Edinburgh Magazine — Volume 55, No. 342, April, 1844
author: Various

quoteFirst: The faint rustling of the heath grew to a wide murmur, the little bells seemed to chime with notes heard only by the innermost spirit, and the gliding dogs were like strange creatures from some shadowy underworld. At times a pheasant would rise and whirl like a rocket from hillock to hollow, and
quoteLast: a rapturous concert began. On one line of trees a colony of nightingales had established themselves near the heart of the waste. First came the low inquiry from the leader; then two or three low twittering answers; then the one long note that lays hold of the nerves and makes the whole being quiver; and then--ah, the passion, the pain, the unutterable delight of the heavenly jargoning when the whole of the little choir begin their magnificent rivalry! The thought of death is gone, the wild and poignant issues of life are softened, and the pulses beat thickly amid the blinding sweetness of the music.
title: The Ethics of Drink and Other Social Questions; Or, Joints In Our Social Armour
author: Runciman, James, 1852-1891

quoteFirst: Rehearsals were called twice a day, morning and evening. As Joyce did not leave the theatre until
quoteLast: he had no time to look in at the familiar billiard-room, and so Noakes and his “penny bloods” were forgotten. On the other hand he spent several of his afternoons with Yvonne, who was delighted with his accounts of himself, and sent him away cheered and sanguine.
title: Derelicts
author: Locke, William John, 1863-1930

quoteFirst: On the next day (25th) the Government made some sort of attempt to open up communications with the besieged, but the attempt was thwarted by Tung-fu-Hsiang’s men, who fired on the bearers of the decree, which curiously enough stated that the Imperial troops were solely to be employed for the defence of the Legations. The only effect which this peaceful attempt had, was to cause a gradual cessation of fire until
quoteLast: , when once more the noisy faction gained the upper hand, and the Chinese again opened fire, this time from behind more substantial cover than they had before been using.
title: The world's navies in the Boxer rebellion (China 1900)
author: Dix, Charles Cabry
